goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Good morning Ameena. 

You said you feel a 76 with high energy this morning — that's a solid rebound from your recent average. **Last night you got 6.8 hours**, which is more than your usual, but the quality wasn't great. Your sleep was pretty fragmented (wake ratio 14.97%), meaning you tossed and turned a lot, and your body's recovery score is still catching up at 52. That tracks with the elevated stress (55) and critically low mood (29) you reported yesterday, which was a sharp drop. 

Here's the interesting part: despite feeling emotionally strained, you had an exceptionally active day — 14,913 steps and 146 intense minutes. That's a lot of output, and it might be contributing to the internal pressure you're feeling. Your social score was a bright spot at 95, which is a real strength to lean into today. 

The big theme for today is **recovery and reconnection**. Your body got more sleep hours but needs deeper quality, and your mind needs a gentler pace after yesterday's high-output, low-mood combo. 

Today is about using your strong social connections as an anchor while you give your system the consistent, calm fuel it's asking for.

assumptions: User is observing Ramadan (based on meal types: suhoor, iftar).
High energy mode (76) may mask underlying fatigue from sleep debt.
Sleep duration (6.8h) is above goal minimum but sleep quality is poor (efficiency 84.82%, wake ratio 14.97%).
Recent positive trend in sleep onset timing (slope_fast -137.38) suggests progress toward earlier bedtime.
Nutrition confidence is moderate (0.71); logged meals are complete but timing is unusual (Ramadan pattern).
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
caution with late caffeine given sleep fragmentation risk

generated_actions.title: Finish Iftar 90 minutes earlier tonight to support sleep continuity
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's last meal was extremely late (23:49), and sleep was fragmented (wake ratio 14.97%, efficiency 84.82%). The cross-domain signal 'late_eating_with_sleep_fragmentation' suggests proximity of eating to sleep may disrupt quality. Today's priority is 'sleep_consistency_recovery'. Moving Iftar 90 minutes earlier directly addresses this pattern and the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep'.

evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 22:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1320), creating a longer buffer before sleep.

assumptions: You can adjust your Iftar timing slightly
generated_actions.description: Yesterday's last meal was at 23:49, aligning with elevated sleep wake ratio (14.97%). Tonight, aim to finish your Ramadan Iftar meal by 22:00 PM. When you sit down for Iftar, set a timer or note the time to ensure you wrap up eating by this earlier cutoff.

generated_actions.title: When you feel 'Annoyed' during work, take a 2-minute social gratitude pause
generated_actions.rationale: Stability status is poor with critically low mood (29) plummeting (-11.21 slope) and elevated stress (55) rising (+6.48 slope), yet social wellbeing is excellent (95). The cross-domain signal 'social_wellbeing_amid_emotional_distress' indicates a resource to leverage. This micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel Annoyed') to disrupt negative emotional spirals and harness your strong social connection as a counterweight.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the social gratitude pause when you felt annoyed today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the brief social gratitude technique at least once when annoyance arose.

assumptions: You can take brief pauses
You have access to your phone or thoughts
generated_actions.description: Yesterday you reported the single emotion 'Annoyed' amid a critically low mood (29). When that feeling arises today, pause and text or think of one person you appreciate. Use your high social score (95) as an anchor to counter the emotional strain.

must_avoid: continuing the annoying task without pause

generated_actions.title: Add a protein-rich component to your Suhoor meal
generated_actions.rationale: Nutrition status is average with adequate protein (121.6g) and calories (2455 kcal) yesterday, but your activity was exceptionally high (146 MVPA minutes). The soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits' suggests reinforcing protein intake today, especially before your 6am basketball practice. Habit-stacking to your Suhoor routine ensures you fuel your high-energy mode without extra planning.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you consciously add extra protein to your Suhoor today?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 120g (building on yesterday's 121.6g) AND user reports intentionally enhancing their morning meal.

assumptions: You have access to additional protein sources
You are preparing Suhoor
generated_actions.description: Yesterday's Suhoor included avocado toast, eggs, turkey bacon, and fava beans—already protein-rich. Today, when preparing your pre-dawn meal, intentionally add or emphasize one extra protein source (like an extra egg, more turkey bacon, or a scoop of yogurt) to further support your high activity day.

generated_actions.title: Do a 3-minute breathing reset right after you get into bed
generated_actions.rationale: Sleep domain status is below_avg with fragmented sleep (wake ratio 14.97%, efficiency 84.82%) and subjective sleep quality critically low (25) and declining sharply (-8.77 slope). This action uses implementation intention ('right after you get into bed') to create a calm transition into sleep, addressing the 'sleep_debt' and 'volatility' risk flags, and directly supports your sleep goal domain and 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete the breathing reset immediately after getting into bed?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports doing the breathing exercise before any other bedtime activity.

assumptions: You can lie down in bed quietly for a few minutes
generated_actions.description: Last night's sleep efficiency was below baseline (84.82%) with elevated wakefulness. Tonight, when you physically get into bed, before reaching for your phone or starting any activity, do 4 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing: inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s. Focus only on your breath.

must_avoid: using your phone or reading immediately

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 7:00 PM 'wind-down start' alarm to protect your bedtime
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Last night's sleep was critically short (2.73h) and fragmented (84.1% efficiency, 15.9% wake ratio). With a severe sleep debt and today's priority being 'sleep_consistency_recovery', an external cue to start winding down extremely early (7:00 PM) directly addresses the 'sleep_debt' and 'mismatch_pattern' risk flags by creating a long, protected buffer for sleep onset, aiming to prevent a repeat of the 2.73h duration.

signal_refs.label: sleep onset earlier than 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes)

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 7:00 PM alarm help you start winding down earlier tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: Sleep onset occurs before 8:00 PM (midnight-based minutes < 1200) AND user reports using the alarm to initiate a screen-free wind-down

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule to start winding down earlier
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Set an alarm on your phone for 7:00 PM. When it goes off, that's your signal to stop work, dim the lights, and begin a 60-minute screen-free wind-down routine (like reading or light tidying). Aim to be in bed by 8:00 PM to maximize sleep opportunity.

must_avoid: starting new tasks after the alarm

previous_day_generated_actions.title: During your first work break, do 3 minutes of gentle neck and shoulder stretches
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery is critically low (physio proxy 35, HRV 85.9) and sharply declining (HRV slope -9.18). Your subjective energy is high (81) but focus is critically low (34) and coping is very low (38), indicating cognitive strain. Gentle movement during a work break promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your system, addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and the 'cognitive_strain_amid_low_activity' cross-domain signal.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 3 minutes of gentle neck and shoulder stretches during your first work break?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing the gentle stretching routine during a morning work break

assumptions: You can take a short break during work
You have space to move your upper body
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When you take your first natural break from work (e.g., after a meeting or task), spend 3 minutes doing slow, gentle neck rolls, shoulder shrugs, and arm circles. Focus on releasing tension, not stretching deeply.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: When you notice your focus dipping, take 2 minutes to look out a window and name 5 things you see
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Cognitive stability is strained: focus was critically low yesterday (34) and declining sharply (-8.86 slope), coping capacity is very low (38) and also declining (-8.56 slope), and emotions included 'Tired'. This micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you notice focus dipping') to disrupt mental fatigue and provide a sensory reset, directly addressing the 'volatility' risk flag and the 'cognitive_strain_amid_low_activity' cross-domain signal.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 'name 5 things' technique when your focus dipped today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the visual grounding technique at least once when they noticed low focus or frustration

assumptions: You have access to a window or distant view
You can pause briefly when focus drops
previous_day_generated_actions.description: If you feel your attention waning or frustration building during work, immediately get up, go to a window, and silently name 5 distinct things you can see outside (e.g., 'tree,' 'cloud,' 'bird'). Breathe slowly while you do this.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Keep caffeine to the morning only today — finish any caffeinated drink by 10:00 AM
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's caffeine intake was 87mg, with a packaged cappuccino logged (75mg). While it was before 2 PM, the soft constraint 'caution with late caffeine given sleep fragmentation risk' is critical given your severely fragmented sleep (wake ratio 15.9%). Restricting caffeine to before 10:00 AM provides a longer clearance window before your early target bedtime, directly supporting your sleep goal domain and 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority.

evaluation.success_definition: No caffeine is logged after 2:00 PM today (caffeine_after_2pm == false), indicating morning-only intake

assumptions: You have control over your caffeine intake
previous_day_generated_actions.description: If you have caffeine today, make sure it's only in the morning and your last sip is before 10:00 AM local time. Stick to water, herbal tea, or decaf after that.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena — you're feeling an 81 this morning, which is great to see.

Let's start with last night: you got **2.7 hours** of sleep. That's less than half your usual, and it was fragmented (you were awake 16% of the time). Your body's recovery signals are critically low — your HRV at 86 ms and physio score at 35 are both sharp declines. This is the freshest signal for your day: your system is running on empty, even if your mood feels high.

What's interesting is that disconnect. You reported high mood (80) and a perfect social score yesterday, while your focus (34) and coping (38) were critically low and still dropping. That cognitive strain happened alongside a big drop in activity — only 7,741 steps and 8 minutes of vigorous movement. When sleep is this short and recovery is this low, your brain has to work much harder just to function, which explains why you felt tired even while feeling happy.

Your strength right now is that positive affective backdrop — you're approaching today with good spirits, which is a real asset when you're physically depleted.

Today is about damage control and gentle support. Your actions focus on protecting tonight's sleep window and giving your strained system micro-breaks without asking more from it.

You've got the right mood for this — use that positive energy to be kind to your body today.

evidence_quotes: My laptop broke down which is very frustrating
I had iftar with my friends which was a fun

meal_descriptions.description: Avocado toast with 2 fried eggs, two spoons of fava beans and a parata bread. Also 3 slices of turkey bacon and some cucumber and carrot slices
A small chicken shawerma wrap and a small beef shawerma in pita bread. A small kofta and a stuffed aubergine. A small zaatar manakeesh.
A 280 kcal peanut bar with 2g of protein and 3 rice stuffed onion jewels
